docs.title: Ecclesiastical History, Book 8
docs.author: Eusebius
docs.excerpt: … , conquered the passionate and intemperate soul of Maximinus by most heroic firmness. Honorable on account of wealth and family and education, she esteemed all of these inferior to chastity .

docs.title: Ecclesiastical History, Book 9
docs.author: Eusebius
docs.excerpt: … the tyrant in the East — a most impious man, if there ever was one, and most hostile to the religion of the God of the universe — being by no means satisfied with its contents, instead of sending the above-quoted decree to the governors under him, gave them verbal commands to relax the war
